sanskrit: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः। प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु॥
transliteration: raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśi-sūryayoḥ / praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṁ nṛṣu
literal_meaning: Taste in water I; O Kaunteya, radiance in moon-and-sun; the sacred syllable OM in all Vedas; sound in ether, manly vigor in humans.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names five ordinary experiences—taste, light, sacred sound, ambient sound, and human agency—to show that the Divine is not elsewhere but already saturating daily life.  The instruction is to shift attention from the object to the luminous quality within it, turning mundane moments into doorways for awakening.
psychological_state: Grounded awe—an open-focus awareness that stabilizes the nervous system by locating the extraordinary inside the ordinary, reducing existential scarcity.
trigger_scenario: You sip lukewarm coffee during a 3 p.m. slump, notice the subtle sweetness, and instead of scrolling your phone you silently think, ‘That taste is the same presence that lights the sun.’  Your shoulders drop; the afternoon feels spacious rather than stale.
anchor_text: Notice the spark within the ordinary—taste, light, sound—and meet God already here.
